content_markdown: Which of the following describes the role of the atmosphere in the greenhouse effect?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: The atmosphere is transparent to all solar radiation.

options.label: B
options.text: The atmosphere absorbs infrared radiation from the ground.

options.label: C
options.text: The atmosphere scatters red light more than blue light.

options.label: D
options.text: Clouds in the atmosphere prevent absorption of infrared radiation.

vibe_explanation: The greenhouse effect occurs because greenhouse gases in the atmosphere are relatively transparent to incoming short-wave solar radiation but absorb long-wave infrared radiation emitted by the Earth's surface, re-radiating it in all directions and warming the planet.
